root_text.sn35.161:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati jīvakambavane.
root_text.sn35.161:1.6: Cakkhuṁ aniccanti yathābhūtaṁ okkhāyati, rūpā aniccāti yathābhūtaṁ okkhāyati, cakkhuviññāṇaṁ aniccanti yathābhūtaṁ okkhāyati, cakkhusamphasso aniccoti yathābhūtaṁ okkhāyati, yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi aniccanti yathābhūtaṁ okkhāyati …pe…
root_text.sn35.161:1.10: yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi aniccanti yathābhūtaṁ okkhāyati.

translation_text.sn35.161:0.3: On Retreat at Jīvaka’s Mango Grove
translation_text.sn35.161:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha in Jīvaka’s Mango Grove.
translation_text.sn35.161:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn35.161:1.4: For a mendicant who meditates in retreat, things become truly clear.
translation_text.sn35.161:1.6: It becomes truly clear that the eye, sights, eye consciousness, and eye contact are impermanent. And it also becomes truly clear that the painful, pleasant, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on eye contact is impermanent. …
translation_text.sn35.161:1.7: It becomes truly clear that the mind, ideas, mind consciousness, and mind contact are impermanent.
translation_text.sn35.161:1.10: And it also becomes truly clear that the painful, pleasant, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact is impermanent.
translation_text.sn35.161:1.12: For a mendicant who meditates in retreat, things become truly clear.”
